---
title: Fan-In Pipelines and Delta Change Data Feed
---

Two capabilities unlock more complex incremental architectures:

1. ingesting from multiple source directories in one run,
2. ingesting changes from an upstream Delta table via CDF.

This library supports both with the same decorator surface.

## Fan-in with multiple source paths

`source` can be a list of directories. Each source contributes one `LazyFrame` argument to the transform function in source order.

If a source has no new files in a run, it is omitted, so argument count can vary across runs.

For dynamic fan-in, define transforms with `*lfs` and concatenate or union them programmatically.

This makes it easy to model multi-region or multi-system landing zones without writing separate pipelines per source.

## Delta as a source with Change Data Feed

Set `file_format="delta"` to ingest from another Delta table using CDF. The first run loads baseline state; subsequent runs process only rows changed since the last tracked source version.

CDF metadata columns are removed before your transform receives the frame, keeping business logic clean.

## Streaming bridge: Arrow record batches -> Polars lazy execution

Under the hood, `record_batch_source.py` wraps a PyArrow `RecordBatchReader` via Polars' `register_io_source` API.

Why this matters:

- batch-by-batch streaming avoids loading full change sets eagerly,
- the integration preserves optimizer features like projection and row limits,
- and CDF ingestion remains aligned with lazy transformation patterns.

The result is a clean abstraction: your transform still sees `LazyFrame` inputs while the library handles low-level source plumbing.
